title: Iliad, Book 6: Hector and Andromache
description: Book 6 of Homer's Iliad: Hector and Andromache.
introduction: Book 6 of the Iliad: Hector and Andromache. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Τρώων δ᾿ οἰώθη καὶ Ἀχαιῶν φύλοπις αἰνή.
translation.text: The dread battle of Trojans and Achaeans was left to itself. Hector returned to the city and found Andromache on the walls with their infant son Astyanax. She begged him not to return to battle: 'Hector, your courage will destroy you. Have pity on your son and on me.' He reached for the child, but the boy shrank back, frightened by the horsehair crest. Hector laughed and took off his helmet, and kissed his son.
